search_queries_used: site:dha.gov.ae sore throat telehealth guideline PDF
site:nice.org.uk sore throat acute antimicrobial recommendations
site:doh.gov.ae sore throat guideline Abu Dhabi
candidate_sources_rejected: standalone antibiotic tables as workflow-wide evidence
rejection_reasons: Patient pages are not clinician guidance.
Dose tables were deliberately excluded from item mappings.
Calculator implementations were not treated as exact guideline evidence.
population_applicability: Partial: exact for adult and paediatric acute sore-throat assessment; not exact for every chronic, malignant, neurological, or noninfectious swallowing complaint.
setting_applicability: Direct for DHA-licensed telehealth; partial for in-person throat, ear, and neck examination.
UAE_applicability: Verified for DHA-licensed telehealth in Dubai, United Arab Emirates.
recency_verification: The current DHA Issue 2 document, effective 2024-04-21 and due for revision 2029-02-21, was reopened on 2026-07-12.
superseded_check: No newer official DHA sore-throat issue was identified.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Reviewed to establish the broad viral and bacterial acute-pharyngitis context; no diagnosis was inferred.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Describes acute pharyngitis as commonly viral and self-limited while requiring systematic assessment for treatable or serious causes.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Reviewed to establish DHA telehealth scope and emergency exclusions; no content was mapped from scope alone.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Defines Dubai telehealth scope and exclusions for emergency and controlled-medication prescribing.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped sore-throat, swallowing, fever, respiratory, tonsillar, node, rash, temperature, and clinician-selected GAS-test fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists swallowing pain, fever, cough and URTI features, tonsillar findings, cervical nodes, rash, and clinician-selected GAS testing context.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped clinician-documented symptom care, medicine decisions, communication, reassessment, and follow-up without doses.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Covers clinician-led symptom care, communication, treatment decisions, reassessment, and follow-up without authorising autonomous medication output.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped drooling, airway, breathing, neck, dehydration, severe-pain, and complication escalation fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists airway, swallowing, breathing, pain, immune compromise, neck stiffness, dehydration, and complication prompts for escalation.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Reviewed for recovery-course context; fixed or diagnosis-specific timing was not added beyond existing exact wording.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: States the adult and paediatric scope and expected recovery or reassessment context for viral and GAS pharyngitis.

unresolved_source_gaps: Broad infected-throat aliases, trismus as a specifically examined negative, otoscopy and mastoid examination, CBC review, hydration-and-rest wording as a combined item, and medication doses remain unsupported.
Streptococcal pharyngitis remains clinician-stated and cannot be inferred from symptoms alone.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.
